info.title: MCPJam API
info.description:
Programmatic access to MCP servers saved in your MCPJam projects — live diagnostics (validate, inspect, export) and operations: call tools, render prompts, run eval suites asynchronously and poll their results, and import OAuth tokens.

**The API is in preview**: the surface may change while we finish the design. Error `code` values are stable; error `message` strings are not. Write clients that ignore unknown response fields.

tags.name: Hosts
tags.description: Project hosts: named model + capability profiles you run chats and eval suites against.

tags.name: Computer environments
tags.description: Custom Computer images: digest-pinned Dockerfile environments your project's computers boot from.

tags.name: Server diagnostics
tags.description: Connect-level health checks against a saved MCP server.

tags.name: Primitives
tags.description: The server's MCP primitives: tools, prompts, and resources.

tags.name: Export
tags.description: Full-server snapshots for diffing and CI.

tags.name: Execution
tags.description: Run the server's primitives: call tools, render prompts.

tags.name: Eval runs
tags.description: Asynchronous eval suite runs: create with 202, poll status, iterations, and traces.

tags.name: OAuth
tags.description: Bring-your-own OAuth: import externally obtained tokens for a server.

tags.name: Chatboxes
tags.description: Read-only access to the chatboxes published from a project: listing, settings, attached servers, and share links.

tags.name: Catalog
tags.description: Discover the resources the other routes operate on: your account, projects, servers, eval suites, and chat sessions.

tags.name: Tunnels
tags.description: Relay tunnels that expose local MCP servers through a public URL, registered as first-class project servers (the `mcpjam tunnel` CLI flow).

get.description: The custom Computer images (digest-pinned Dockerfile environments) saved in the project — your own personal drafts plus the project-shared ones.

post.description: Create a personal-draft environment from a Dockerfile and respond `201` with its detail. Build it (`POST .../build`) before a computer can boot from it. Guest callers are denied (a write).

get.description: One environment's Dockerfile, sharing, and latest build. An environment that exists but belongs to a different project reads as `404 NOT_FOUND`.

patch.description: Edit an environment's name and/or Dockerfile. Re-build it for the changes to take effect on a computer. Guest callers are denied (a write).

delete.description: Permanently delete an environment. Computers booted from it fall back to the base image. Deleting a project-shared environment requires project admin. Bodyless — any field is rejected. Guest callers are denied (a write).

get.description: An environment's builds, newest first, each with its status and capped log preview.

post.description: Trigger a build of the environment's image and respond `202`. The build runs asynchronously — poll the builds list for status. Bodyless. Guest callers are denied (a write).

post.summary: Share an environment with the project
post.description: Promote a personal-draft environment to a project-shared one. Requires project admin. Bodyless. Guest callers are denied (a write).

post.summary: Boot your computer from an environment
post.description: Attach the environment to the caller's computer, which re-provisions it from the pinned image (installed files are wiped). The environment must have a `ready` build, and the computer provider must match the build's. Bodyless. Guest callers are denied (a write).

200.description: The environment was attached; the computer is re-provisioning.

post.summary: Reset your computer to its image
post.description: Reset the caller's computer back to its current image, wiping mutable state. Bodyless. Guest callers are denied (a write).

get.description: The hosts saved in the project — the named model + capability profiles you attach to chats and eval suites. Returns the `id`s the host detail/update/delete routes take.

post.summary: Create a host (from a template or a full config)
post.description:
Creates a host in the project and responds `201` with the new host's detail. Seed the host config one of two ways: pass `template` (a built-in template id, optional `theme`) to seed server-side from the same presets the inspector UI uses, OR pass `config` (a full host config v2). Exactly one of `template` or `config` is required.

Guest callers are denied (host creation is a write).

get.description: One host's full settings, including its resolved host config. A host that exists but belongs to a different project reads as `404 NOT_FOUND`.

patch.description: Edit a host's display name and/or its host config. Only the fields you pass change. Guest callers are denied (a write).

delete.description: Permanently delete a host from the project. Pass `{ "force": true }` to delete a host that is still referenced (e.g. by an eval suite). Guest callers are denied (a write).

force.description: Delete even if the host is still referenced.

post.description: Connects to the server, initializes the MCP session, and returns a connection snapshot (server info, negotiated capabilities). The same check the hosted inspector runs when you connect a server.
Runs the full doctor workflow — probe → connect → initialize → capabilities → primitives — and returns a step-by-step report. The richest signal for "is this server healthy, and why not."

200.description: Doctor report. `status` summarizes the outcome; per-step detail is in `checks`.

post.description: Lightweight, no MCP connection: reports whether the server is configured to require an OAuth grant.
Returns the server's tools as a collection page. Each item is the MCP tool definition (`name`, `description`, `inputSchema`, ...).

items.name: create_issue
items.description: Create a new issue in the tracker

post.description: Returns the server's prompts as a collection page.
Returns the server's resources as a collection page.
Reads a single resource by URI and returns its contents.

uri.description: The resource URI, exactly as returned by the resources list.

post.description: Lists tools, resources, and prompts in one call and returns a single JSON snapshot — handy for diffing a server's surface over time in CI.

Executes a tool on the server and returns the MCP `CallToolResult` directly. Tool-level failures (`isError: true` in the result) are **successful calls** — the server answered; only transport/auth errors use the error envelope.

toolName.description: Tool name, exactly as returned by the tools list.

parameters.description: Tool arguments matching the tool's `inputSchema`. Defaults to `{}`.

200.description: The MCP `CallToolResult`. Check `isError` for tool-level failures.

post.description: Renders a prompt with arguments and returns the MCP `GetPromptResult` directly (`description?`, `messages`).

promptName.description: Prompt name, exactly as returned by the prompts list.

arguments.description: Prompt arguments. String, number, and boolean values.

post.description:
Creates a suite run from an existing `suiteId` (rerun) and/or inline `tests`, then **detaches execution and responds `202` immediately** with the `runId`. Validation and quota errors surface on this request; poll `GET /eval-runs/{runId}` for progress. The run appears live in the hosted UI Runs tab, tagged `source: "api"`.

A bare `suiteId` with no inline tests reruns the suite as configured. Per-organization concurrency is capped (default 2 concurrent runs); exceeding it returns `429` with `details.reason: "CONCURRENT_RUN_LIMIT"`.

steps.prompt: Use the echo tool to say hi

202.description: Run created; execution continues in the background.

get.description: Run status, result, and summary. Poll until `status` is terminal (`completed`, `failed`, or `cancelled`).
Per-iteration results: actual tool calls, structured token usage, and latency. Cursor-paginated.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor from a previous response's `nextCursor`.

get.description: Full trace envelope for one iteration: conversation messages, expected-vs-actual tool call analysis, and spans. The shape is rich and may evolve — treat it as an open document. Returns `404` with `details.reason: "TRACE_NOT_AVAILABLE"` when the iteration finished without a stored trace.

schema.description: Trace document: `messages`, `prompts` (per-prompt expected/actual tool-call analysis), `spans`, and more.

get.description: One row per authored test step, in order, with `status` (`ok`/`fail`/`skipped`/`pending`), a `reason`, and any `evidence` (screenshots, replay-video offset, widget tool calls). The fastest way to see which step failed and why. Unlike `/trace`, a missing trace is not a `404` — step verdicts still return, just without evidence.

200.description: The ordered step results as a page envelope.

items.description: Authored steps in order with their verdict.

evidence.description: Optional, omitted when the step produced none.

post.description: Request cancellation of an in-flight run; marks the run and its pending/running iterations `cancelled`. A no-op success when the run is already cancelled; returns `409` when the run already reached a terminal status (`completed`/`failed`/`timed_out`).

409.description: The run already finished and cannot be cancelled.

get.description: Recent runs for a suite, newest first.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum runs to return, 1–100. Defaults to 25.

post.description:
Stores OAuth tokens you obtained yourself (e.g. via the SDK's `runOAuthLogin` — interactive loopback, headless, or client-credentials) for this server, scoped to your user, project, and server. Subsequent API calls against the server inject the stored access token automatically, and `401`s from the server trigger a server-side refresh — no further caller involvement.

This closes the loop after an `OAUTH_REQUIRED` error.

get.description: The user behind the API key (keys act as their creator, scoped to one organization).
Projects the caller can access. API keys only ever see projects inside the key's organization.
The MCP servers saved in the project — the `serverId`s every other route takes. STDIO command/args/env and raw headers are never exposed.
Eval suites in the project with latest-run summaries and pass-rate trends — the `suiteId`s the eval-run routes take.

post.summary: Create an eval suite (author-only, does not run)
post.description:
Creates a runnable eval suite — the suite record plus its test cases — and responds `201` **synchronously**, WITHOUT executing anything. Use this to author a suite, then run it later with `POST /eval-runs` (passing the returned `suiteId`).

This is distinct from `POST /eval-runs`, which creates a run and **detaches execution**, responding `202` with a `runId`. There is no concurrency cap here (no run is started).

The body uses an ergonomic authoring shape: a suite-level default `model` (and optional `provider`) applies to every test unless the test overrides it; `provider` is derived from a `provider/model` id when neither is supplied. Each test's case body is an ordered `steps` array (prompt / toolCall / interact / assert).

Guest callers are denied (suite creation is a write).

steps.prompt: List the files in the project root.

get.description: Chat sessions visible to the caller (personal + project-shared merged). Top-level rather than project-nested because `projectId` is an optional filter, not an owning scope.

200.description: One page of chat sessions, newest activity first.

get.description: The chatboxes published from this project — name, access mode, attached servers, and share link. Read-only.
One chatbox's full read-only settings: model, system prompt, tool-approval policy, attached servers, and share link. A chatbox that exists but belongs to a different project reads as `404 NOT_FOUND`.

post.summary: Create (or revive) a relay tunnel for a named project server
post.description:
Registers a server record named `name` if missing, mints a relay tunnel grant for it, and **persists the tunnel bearer URL (including the plaintext `?k=` secret) onto the server record's `url`** so evals and chatboxes can target the tunnel like any remote server. The plaintext persistence is a deliberate trade-off of the current tunnel MVP — the backend otherwise stores only a hash of the secret — mitigated by rotation: **every call rotates the secret, revokes the previous grant at the edge (disconnecting any live tunnel session for the server), and updates the stored URL**, so re-calling this route is also the rotation/recovery path.

The caller hosts the tunnel itself: connect a WebSocket to `relayWsUrl` (subprotocol `mcpjam-tunnel.v1`, `Authorization: Bearer <connectToken>`) and serve the relayed requests — this is what `mcpjam tunnel` does. When the host disconnects, the server record stays and calls to the public URL fail fast at the edge.

201.description: Tunnel grant minted and the server record updated.

post.description: Revokes the grant at the control plane and edge: the public URL stops working immediately and any live tunnel session is disconnected. The server record — including its now-dead `url` — is intentionally left untouched, so the next create revives the tunnel with the same slug.

200.description: Grant revoked; the server record is unchanged.

bearerAuth.description: MCPJam API key (`sk_…`). Create one at [Settings → API keys](https://app.mcpjam.com/settings/api-keys). Guest sessions cannot use the API, and API keys cannot manage other API keys.

projectId.name: projectId
projectId.description: ID of the hosted project that contains the server.

serverId.name: serverId
serverId.description: ID of the server inside the project.

runId.name: runId
runId.description: Eval run ID, as returned by `POST /eval-runs`.

iterationId.name: iterationId
iterationId.description: Iteration ID, as returned by the run's iterations list.

suiteId.name: suiteId
suiteId.description: Eval suite ID, as returned by `POST /eval-runs`.

chatboxId.name: chatboxId
chatboxId.description: Chatbox ID, as returned by the project's chatbox list.

hostId.name: hostId
hostId.description: Host ID, as returned by the project's host list.

environmentId.name: environmentId
environmentId.description: Computer environment ID, as returned by the project's environment list.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor from a previous response's `nextCursor`. Don't parse it.

code.description: Stable, machine-readable error code. New codes may be added over time; treat unknown codes as non-retryable failures unless the HTTP status says otherwise.

message.description: Human-readable description. May change between releases — don't match on it.

initInfo.description: MCP initialization snapshot: server info, negotiated protocol version, and capabilities. `null` if the server connected but initialization data was unavailable.

DoctorReport.description: Step-by-step health report from the probe → connect → initialize → capabilities workflow.

status.description: Overall outcome. `partial` means the server connected but some primitive listings failed.

probe.description: Transport-level probe result (HTTP reachability, protocol hints), or `null` when skipped.

checks.description: Per-step status. Each check is `{ status: ok | error | skipped, detail }`.

error.description: Terminal error, or `null` when the workflow completed.

useOAuth.description: Whether the server is configured to require an OAuth grant.

serverUrl.description: The server's URL, or `null` for non-HTTP transports.

Tool.description: MCP tool definition, as returned by the server.

inputSchema.description: JSON Schema for the tool's arguments.

outputSchema.description: JSON Schema for the tool's structured output, when declared.

Prompt.description: MCP prompt definition, as returned by the server.

Resource.description: MCP resource descriptor, as returned by the server.

blob.description: Base64-encoded payload, present for binary resources.

nextCursor.description: Opaque cursor for the next page. Omitted on the last page.
Opaque cursor for the next page. Omitted on the last page.
Opaque cursor for the next page. Omitted on the last page.

CallToolResult.description: MCP tool execution result, returned verbatim from the server.

structuredContent.description: Structured output matching the tool's `outputSchema`, when declared.

isError.description: `true` when the **tool** reported a failure. The HTTP call still succeeds — the server answered.

GetPromptResult.description: MCP prompt render result, returned verbatim from the server.

EvalTestStep.description: One authored test step (the unified test model). `kind` discriminates: `prompt` is a user message (model turn); `toolCall` is a deterministic, model-free tool call; `interact` is one pure widget action; `assert` is an assertion (a `Predicate` like `toolCalledWith` / `widgetRendered`, or a DOM `WidgetAssertion`).

serverName.description: Server that owns the tool (`kind: toolCall`).

EvalTestCase.description: Inline eval test case. The case body is an ordered `steps` array (the unified test model).

steps.description: Ordered test steps. The first `prompt` step is the case query; `toolCalledWith` asserts are the expected tool calls; a single model-free `toolCall` step is a render-check.

runs.description: Iterations to execute for this case.

model.description: Model ID. Hosted-catalog ids use `provider/name` form (e.g. `anthropic/claude-haiku-4.5`) and run on org credits; provider-native ids require a matching `modelApiKeys` entry (BYOK). Unknown models are rejected with VALIDATION_ERROR at create time.

isNegativeTest.description: When `true`, the case passes if NO tools are called.

EvalSuiteCreateRequest.description: Author-only suite-create body. A suite-level default `model` (and optional `provider`) applies to every test unless the test overrides it.

serverIds.description: Servers (by canonical project ID) the suite's cases run against.

model.description: Suite-level default model id (e.g. `anthropic/claude-haiku-4.5`). Used for any test that omits `model`.

provider.description: Optional suite-level default provider. When omitted, the provider is derived from a `provider/model` id.

tags.description: Accepted for forward-compat; not persisted today (no-op).

tests.description: Test cases to create in the suite.

steps.description: Ordered test steps (the unified test model). The first `prompt` step is the case query; `toolCalledWith` asserts are the expected tool calls; a single model-free `toolCall` step is a render-check.

runs.description: Iterations to execute for this case. Defaults to 1.

model.description: Per-case model override. Defaults to the suite-level `model`.

isNegativeTest.description: When `true`, the case passes if NO tools are called.

servers.description: The servers attached to the suite. `name` is present when supplied.

caseUpsert.description: Per-case create outcomes. Partial failures don't abort the suite; an all-failed new suite is rejected with VALIDATION_ERROR.

EvalRunCreateRequest.description: Two valid shapes: `suiteId` (rerun an existing suite, optionally upserting inline `tests` into it), or `suiteName` + `tests` + `serverIds` (create a new suite and run it). Inline `tests` alone — without a `suiteId` or a `suiteName` — are rejected with `VALIDATION_ERROR`.

suiteId.description: Existing suite to rerun. A bare `suiteId` with no `tests` reruns the suite exactly as configured.

suiteName.description: Name for a new suite. Required (non-empty) when no `suiteId` is given.

tests.description: Inline test cases to upsert into the suite before running.

serverIds.description: Servers (by ID) the run connects to. Required when creating a new suite; optional on reruns — when omitted, the run connects the suite's saved server selection (the set its snapshot references). A rerun of a suite with no saved selection is rejected with `VALIDATION_ERROR` (`details.reason: "NO_SAVED_SERVER_SELECTION"`).

modelApiKeys.description: Optional per-provider model API keys (e.g. `{ "anthropic": "sk-ant-…" }`). Falls back to your organization's configured providers when omitted.

iterationOverride.description: Override the per-case `runs` count for this run only.

servers.description: The servers the run connects to — explicit or derived from the suite's saved selection. `name` is present when known (always, on the derived path).

caseUpsert.description: Per-case upsert outcomes for inline `tests`. Partial failures don't abort the run.

nextCursor.description: Opaque cursor for the next page. Omitted on the last page.

serverUrl.description: The MCP server URL the tokens were obtained for.

oauthResourceUrl.description: OAuth protected-resource URL, when it differs from `serverUrl`.

clientInformation.description: OAuth client used to obtain the tokens. Optional, but without it server-side refresh cannot run — always include it when you provide a `refresh_token`.

expires_in.description: Seconds until the access token expires.

role.description: Caller's role on the project, when resolved.

ProjectServer.description: A saved MCP server, projected toward the hosted (HTTP) shape. STDIO command/args/env and raw headers are never exposed.

url.description: Endpoint for HTTP-transport servers; `null` for stdio.

passRateTrend.description: Recent runs' pass rates, oldest first.

nextCursor.description: Pass as `before` to fetch the next page. Omitted on the last page.

expiresAt.description: Epoch milliseconds when the stored access token expires, or `null` if unknown.

ChatboxLink.description: Share link for the chatbox. The URL embeds the access token; visible to any caller who can read the chatbox (the same audience that sees it in the hosted UI).

ChatboxServer.description: A server attached to the chatbox. Chatboxes attach HTTP servers only, so `url` is the server's endpoint.

Chatbox.description: Summary of a published chatbox, as returned by the list endpoint.

hostStyle.description: Chat surface style the chatbox renders (e.g. `claude`, `chatgpt`).

hostId.description: The named host the chatbox publishes (hosts and chatboxes are 1:1).

ChatboxDetail.description: A chatbox's full read-only settings: the summary fields plus the host execution config and resolved servers.

hostStyle.description: Chat surface style the chatbox renders (e.g. `claude`, `chatgpt`).

hostId.description: The named host the chatbox publishes (hosts and chatboxes are 1:1).

name.description: Server name to register the tunnel under. Reusing an existing server's name points that record at the tunnel: its `url` is overwritten and stdio records are converted to `transportType: "http"`.

TunnelGrant.description: Everything the caller needs to host the tunnel connection. **Treat the whole object as a credential**: `url` embeds the plaintext `?k=` bearer secret and `connectToken` authenticates the relay WebSocket.

existed.description: True when a server record with this name already existed (its config now points at the tunnel).

previousUrl.description: The record's previous URL, present when it existed with a different one (it has been overwritten).

previousTransportType.description: The record's previous transport, present when it existed (a `stdio` record has been converted to `http`).

slug.description: Stable public subdomain slug; survives re-creates for the same server.

url.description: Public tunnel URL including the `?k=` bearer secret. Also persisted on the server record's `url`.

connectToken.description: Bearer for the relay edge WebSocket handshake.

relayWsUrl.description: Relay edge WebSocket endpoint to connect to.

secretVersion.description: Monotonic secret version; each create bumps it.

Host.description: A host in a project, as returned by the host list.

hostConfigId.description: ID of the content-addressed host config this host points at.

serverCount.description: Number of MCP servers attached to the host config.

HostDetail.description: A host plus its resolved host config (model, capabilities, host context, MCP profile).

config.description: Host config v2 DTO. Opaque object; shape mirrors the inspector's host editor.

HostCreateRequest.description: Create a host from a built-in template OR a full host config. Exactly one of `template` or `config` is required.

name.description: Display name for the new host.

template.description: Built-in template id to seed the host config from.

theme.description: Theme stamped into the seeded config (template only).

config.description: Full host config v2, used verbatim (alternative to `template`).

HostUpdateRequest.description: Edit a host. Provide at least one of `name` or `config`; only the fields you pass change.

ComputerEnvironmentBuild.description: One build of an environment's image.

baseImageDigests.description: Resolved sha256 digest of every FROM base (the reproducibility pin).

logPreview.description: Capped, ANSI-stripped tail of the build log.

ComputerEnvironment.description: A project's custom Computer image: a digest-pinned Dockerfile plus its latest build.

sharing.description: `user` = a personal draft; `project` = shared with the whole project.

ComputerEnvironmentCreateRequest.description: Create an environment from a Dockerfile. The Dockerfile must start FROM an allowlisted official base pinned by @sha256 digest; only FROM + RUN are supported.

name.description: Display name for the new environment; must be non-empty after trimming.

ComputerEnvironmentUpdateRequest.description: Edit an environment. Provide at least one of `name` or `dockerfile`; only the fields you pass change.

name.description: New display name; must be non-empty after trimming.

ComputerEnvironmentBuildStarted.description: A build was accepted (202). It runs in the background — poll the builds list for status.

reused.description: True when an identical prior build was reused instead of rebuilding.

status.description: The computer's lifecycle status after attaching (it re-provisions).

Unauthorized.description: Missing, invalid, revoked, or orphaned key (`UNAUTHORIZED`) — or the **target MCP server** needs an OAuth grant (`OAUTH_REQUIRED`), which is a property of the server, not your key.

oauthRequired.summary: Target server needs an OAuth grant

Forbidden.description: Key is valid but not allowed to do this.

example.message: You do not have access to this project

FeatureNotSupported.description: The target server doesn't support this MCP capability.

RateLimited.description: Per-key rate limit exceeded (60 requests/minute sustained, bursts up to 10). Honor `Retry-After` and back off with jitter.

example.message: API key rate limit exceeded. Slow down and retry.

ServerUnreachable.description: Could not connect to the target MCP server.

Timeout.description: The target MCP server connected but didn't respond in time.
